notes: 12 Jul 2026: Beat Switzerland 3-1 AET (Arrowhead Stadium, Kansas City) — Mac Allister opened 10', Ndoye levelled after the hour, Álvarez struck from range in extra time (112') and Lautaro Martínez added a late clincher (121'); Switzerland's Breel Embolo was sent off 72' for a second yellow after a VAR review overturned an initial card against Paredes. Romero played the full first period of extra time (105') before a fitness-management substitution — no fresh injury reported, reads as rotation after his R16 cramp scare. Face England in the semi-final, Atlanta, 16 Jul.

12 Jul 2026 (semi-final watch): Almada, Lautaro Martínez and José Manuel López were each booked in the Switzerland QF (first caution of the tournament for each) but single yellows are wiped clean ahead of the semi-final round, so none carries a suspension risk vs England. H2H: Argentina lead 2-1 across three prior WC knockout meetings with England — 1986 QF ('Hand of God'/Maradona's Goal of the Century) and 1998 R16 (2-2 AET, won 4-3 on penalties) to England's 1966 QF win; first-ever WC semi-final meeting between the sides.

13 Jul 2026: Messi took a knock/cut near his right eye (contact with Xhaka) in the Switzerland QF, bled but continued after treatment; some swelling risk but expected fit for the England semi-final (15 Jul... correction: 16 Jul, Atlanta). No other fitness concerns in the squad.

14 Jul 2026: Widely predicted Argentina XI vs England: Emiliano Martínez; Molina, Romero, Lisandro Martínez, Tagliafico; De Paul, Paredes, Enzo Fernández, Mac Allister; Messi, Lautaro Martínez — Messi in his usual free No.10 role with Lautaro (over Álvarez) tipped to lead the line after his extra-time winner vs Switzerland. No fresh fitness concerns beyond the healed eye knock.

15 Jul 2026: SF-eve reports diverge on XI details — some outlets (Yahoo/AP wire) list Nicolás Otamendi/Medina alongside Cristian Romero uncertainty at CB and Julián Álvarez ahead of Lautaro Martínez up top, while others (ESPN, ours 14 Jul) still have Romero-L.Martínez CB and Lautaro leading the line; De Paul-Paredes-Enzo-Mac Allister midfield four and Messi in the free role are consistent across all reports. No new injuries — Messi's eye knock fully resolved, full squad available.

15 Jul 2026 (SF-eve, later): Cristian Romero and Leandro Paredes both CONFIRMED available for the England semi-final (TyC Sports/Gaston Edul) — Romero's HT-of-ET withdrawal vs Switzerland was cramp-related, not structural; he said postgame he'd not played 90+ mins in 3 months and the heat caught up with him, hence Otamendi's sub. No fitness doubts remain in the squad. Also: World Cup yellow-card amnesty wipes all bookings before the semis, so no team has a suspension for this tie (Bellingham, De Paul, Enzo Fernández etc. all clear); only a red card here would cost the final.

16 Jul 2026: Beat England 2-1 (Atlanta) to reach the 19 Jul final vs Spain — fell behind to Anthony Gordon's 55' finish, then Messi engineered a stunning 7-minute turnaround, teeing up Enzo Fernandez's 85' equalizer and Lautaro Martinez's 92' header winner. Three yellows in a physical tie (Lisandro Martinez, Romero, plus England's Anderson) but no fresh injuries. Third final in four tournaments.

17 Jul 2026: Final team news (vs Spain, MetLife Stadium, 19 Jul) — Cristian Romero had cramps late vs England but played the full 90 and is fine; Leandro Paredes (subbed 64' vs England) has no fitness concern. Fully fit squad; no suspension risk (yellow-card amnesty resets after group stage AND after the QF, so the semi-final bookings for Lisandro Martinez, Romero and De Paul don't carry into the final).

18 Jul 2026: Final-eve — no change to the 17 Jul team news; Romero and Paredes both confirmed fine, fully fit squad. Scaloni's main concern flagged by press is fatigue rather than injuries, having played two extra-time knockout matches (R16 and QF) versus Spain's more comfortable route.

19 Jul 2026: Final-day team news — Facundo Medina (calf, out since the Cape Verde R32 tie) remains a race against time for the final; no other fresh issues reported after the Argentina semi win over England.

19 Jul 2026: Final pre-match — fully fit squad, no fresh injury concerns beyond the standing Medina calf doubt. Messi (39) set to start alongside Alvarez, in what may be his last Argentina appearance.

19 Jul 2026: Runners-up — lost the final 1-0 AET to Spain, denied a second-straight FIFA World Cup for Argentina. Held out for 105 minutes largely on defensive resilience (2 shots, 35% possession) before Ferran Torres' 106' goal, then played the last stretch a man light after Enzo Fernandez's 2nd yellow. Facundo Medina's calf did not stop him from featuring (20 mins off the bench). This was Lionel Messi's likely final World Cup appearance (90 mins played), finishing the tournament tied for the Golden Boot race lead at 8 goals.

notes: 25 Jun 2026: Dominant 3-0 win vs Scotland. Vinícius Jr scored twice (7', 45+3'); Matheus Cunha added third (60'). 21 shots, 4.46 xG, 54% possession. Scotland had 14 shots (1.13 xG) but no reward. Brazil win Group C.

25 Jun 2026: Neymar's emotional comeback — entered 76th min vs Scotland, first Brazil app in 981 days (last played Oct 2023, ACL). 20-min cameo; encouraging return ahead of knockouts. Vinícius Jr was outstanding (2 goals, relentless pressing), Cunha completed 3-0.

28 Jun 2026: Group C winners. Vinicius Jr tournament standout (4G 1A). Neymar returned off bench vs Scotland — fitness building. Raphinha hamstring re-injury is key concern for Japan R32. Without Raphinha/Rodrygo, Brazil's wing attack relies on Vinicius + Savinho/Endrick options.
29 Jun 2026: Face Japan R32 tonight (1AM SGT Jun 30). Raphinha still out (hamstring); Rayan expected to start wide. Vinicius Jr key vs Japan's organised defensive block.

29 Jun 2026: Won R32 M76 vs Japan 2-1 (Casemiro 56', Martinelli 90+5'). Came from behind after Sano opener (29'). 69% poss, 19 shots — dominant but conceded on counter. Martinelli sub proved decisive. Casemiro booked (YC). Advance to R16 M91.

30 Jun 2026: R32 vs Japan (w 2-1): Japan led 1-0 HT (Sano 29'). Casemiro equalised (56'); sub Martinelli winner (90+5'). Without Raphinha. 69% poss, 19 sh, 7 sot. Had to dig deep; Vinicius Jr quiet but ran channels. Casemiro + Danilo Luiz booked — 1 yellow each in KO phase, suspension risk R16. R16 vs Ivory Coast/Norway winner ~5 Jul.

2 Jul 2026: Face Norway in R16 M91 (East Rutherford NJ, Jul 6, ~4am SGT). Norway beat Ivory Coast 2-1 on Nusa's wondergoal and a late Haaland winner — Haaland's aerial/pace threat vs Brazil's back line is the key individual battle.

3 Jul 2026: With Paqueta ruled out, Ancelotti expected to reshuffle midfield and bring Neymar into the starting XI vs Norway — his fitness had been in the balance, now leaning toward starting. Casemiro went down late (90+2') vs Japan, severity unconfirmed, one to monitor.
4 Jul 2026: Danilo (elbow) and Casemiro (cynical foul) both booked vs Japan — either one is suspended from the QF if booked again vs Norway.
5 Jul 2026: Raphinha ruled out of the Norway R16 clash (hamstring not recovered in time). Casemiro (booked vs Japan, suspension risk if booked again) growing optimism/predicted to start regardless.

5 Jul 2026: Paqueta's injury confirmed as a grade-2 thigh strain via scan. Casemiro (forced off late vs Japan) widely expected to start regardless. Endrick tipped to start at ST in Paqueta/Raphinha's absence with Rayan wide, pushing Matheus Cunha into the No.10 role vs Norway. Danilo and Casemiro (both booked vs Japan) remain a yellow from a QF ban if booked again.

6 Jul 2026: Ancelotti's side eliminated 1-2 by Norway in R16 (their earliest World Cup exit skein continues vs European opposition) — Bruno Guimaraes had a first-half penalty saved by Nyland, Neymar did convert a late spot-kick for his first goal of the tournament but Haaland's 79'/90' brace (header, then a low finish from range beating Gabriel Magalhaes) proved decisive; Brazil created enough to score more but were undone defending crosses/second balls late on

notes: 10 Jul 2026: Confirmed XI news for the Belgium QF (SoFi Stadium, LA) — Nico Williams fit again but starts from the bench, Alex Baena fills the left-wing slot instead (Unai Simon; Cucurella, Cubarsi, Laporte, Porro; Rodri, Pedri, Dani Olmo; Baena, Oyarzabal, Yamal). H2H: Spain lead 12-5-6 across 23 meetings and are unbeaten vs Belgium in any competition since Euro 1980.

11 Jul 2026: Beat Belgium 2-1 in the QF (SoFi Stadium) — Fabián Ruiz opened the scoring 30', De Ketelaere levelled 41' before HT, Mikel Merino's 88th-min rebound (off backup keeper Lammens after Courtois's injury exit) settled it. Cubarsí and Laporte both booked but cards reset before the semi. Spain dominated territorially (68% poss, 17 shots to 5) and face France in the semi-final, Arlington, Jul 15.

12 Jul 2026 (semi-final watch): Cubarsí and Laporte's bookings in the Belgium QF were each a first caution of the tournament and — like the rest of the squad's — are wiped clean ahead of the semi-final, so no suspension risk vs France. Nico Williams (adductor/groin) was back among the substitutes for the Belgium QF, fully recovered but still short of full match sharpness. Squad otherwise a clean bill of health heading into the semi.

13 Jul 2026: Nico Williams confirmed fully recovered from the Uruguay-match leg injury — got 12 minutes as a sub (78') in the Belgium QF, on track for a fuller role vs France. Daniel Muñoz managing a calf setback but expected available off the bench. No suspensions — full squad available for the France semi-final.

14 Jul 2026: Víctor Muñoz (Liverpool signing, out since a pre-tournament calf/muscle injury and an unused sub in all 5 games so far) has been cleared after a 7-week layoff and is in contention to make his World Cup debut off the bench vs France — squad otherwise a clean bill of health, Nico Williams and Yeremy Pino both fully available.

14 Jul 2026 (update): De la Fuente's rotation trend points to Alex Baena and Fabián Ruiz starting ahead of Ferran Torres and Pedri for the France semi. Widely predicted XI: Simón; Porro, Cubarsí, Laporte, Cucurella; Rodri, Fabián; Yamal, Olmo, Baena; Oyarzábal — Oyarzábal (4 tournament goals) leading the line over Ferran Torres.

15 Jul 2026: Beat France 2-0 in the semi-final (Arlington) to reach the 19 Jul final — Yamal drew a Digne foul for Oyarzabal's 22' penalty, then Porro finished a Dani Olmo give-and-go (58'). France's William Saliba went off injured (non-contact, 30') but that's a France concern, not Spain's; Spain's own squad picked up no fresh knocks (only Cucurella booked, wiped before the final). Await the England-Argentina SF2 (16 Jul) for the final opponent.

17 Jul 2026: Final team news (vs Argentina, MetLife Stadium, 19 Jul) — Pedro Porro carries a marginal muscle knock from the France semi but is expected fit; Yamal's late limping scare vs France was not serious. Squad otherwise a clean bill of health; no suspension risk (FIFA's yellow-card amnesty resets after group stage AND after the QF, so no one carries a caution into the final).

18 Jul 2026: Nico Williams (abductor issue earlier in the tournament) is fully fit and available but remains a bench option — De la Fuente sticking with Alex Baena in the front three for the Argentina final; not an injury doubt, a tactical/form call. Predicted XI unchanged from the semi: Simon; Porro, Cubarsi, Laporte, Cucurella; Rodri, Fabian Ruiz, Olmo; Yamal, Oyarzabal, Baena (4-3-3).

19 Jul 2026: Final-day team news — Lamine Yamal missed Thursday's training with a bandaged left thigh but is not considered a serious doubt; Pedro Porro (muscular knock from the France semi) also expected to be fit. Yeremy Pino remains Spain's only confirmed absentee (shoulder, out since groups).

19 Jul 2026: Final pre-match — Yamal trending fit after skipping full training Thu, played full 90 in SF vs France with left-leg discomfort but cleared to start; Porro (hamstring fatigue) also expected fit. Pino out (shoulder, since group stage).

19 Jul 2026: WORLD CHAMPIONS — beat Argentina 1-0 AET in the final. Goalless and cagey through 90 (Spain dominated the ball, 65% poss/20 shots/12 on target vs Argentina's 2 shots, but couldn't break down a deep block), decided by substitute Ferran Torres' 106' winner. Argentina finished with 10 men after Enzo Fernandez's second yellow. Yamal completes the tournament as Player of the Tournament-calibre with Spain conceding freely across the knockouts; Lamine Yamal and Pedro Porro both played through their pre-match fitness doubts without issue.

19 Jun 2026: Crushed 0-6 by Canada. Homam Ahmed red 33 (denying Buchanan), Madibo red (VAR upgrade). With 9 men, conceded 4 more. Jonathan David hat-trick tormented depleted Qatar.

19 Jun 2026: Thrashed 0-6 by Canada. 2 straight red cards (Homam Ahmed, Assim Madibo) reduced to 9 men. 0 shots on target from 2 attempts, 22% possession. Qatar effectively eliminated; been outscored 7-1 in two games after 1-1 vs Switzerland.

notes: 3 Jul 2026: Beat Algeria 2-0 despite Algeria having 56% possession — Yakin set up to absorb pressure and counter (Swiss xG 2.52 v 0.73), first Swiss WC knockout win in 88 years. Embolo opener 10', Ndoye 2nd goal 48 seconds into 2nd half, Johan Manzambi (20) assisted the opener — youngest player since 1966 with 5 goal involvements in a single WC. Silvan Widmer played through a hip/thigh knock (missed a pre-match training session) — no in-match aggravation, but a status worth checking again before R16.
5 Jul 2026: Silvan Widmer (hip/thigh knock carried through the Algeria win) is back in full training, trending toward fit for the Colombia R16 tie.

6 Jul 2026: R16 vs Colombia (Vancouver, 7 Jul) — the joint-tightest tie of the round per models (~48-52%). Front three of Manzambi/Vargas/Embolo have combined for 7 goals this WC; Xhaka has bossed midfield, Kobel/Akanji/Ricardo Rodriguez solid at the back. Aebischer (muscle) still a doubt, training individually.
7 Jul 2026: Luca Jaquez (muscle) has emerged as a fresh fitness doubt at centre-back alongside Aebischer ahead of the Colombia R16 tie.

7 Jul 2026: Beat Colombia 4-3 on penalties after a goalless 120 minutes in the R16 (Vancouver) to reach the World Cup quarter-finals for the first time since 1954 — Lucumi hit the crossbar and Campaz shot over in extra time for Colombia; in the shootout Davinson Sanchez struck the bar, Kobel saved Cucho Hernandez's kick, and Ruben Vargas converted the winner. Aebischer and Jaquez (both muscle doubts) didn't feature. Xhaka, Zakaria and Muheim were booked but it's wiped clean entering the QF vs Argentina (Kansas City, Jul 12) under FIFA's 2026 card-reset rule.

7 Jul 2026: QF vs Argentina locked in (Kansas City, Jul 12) — the holders looked shaky in their R16, conceding twice to Egypt before a stoppage-time winner. Switzerland need Xhaka to control tempo and their front three to punish any high-line lapses; Aebischer/Jaquez fitness for a fourth game in 12 days is the key concern.

8 Jul 2026: Johan Manzambi (knee, MRI-confirmed) missed the Colombia R16 shootout win entirely — a big loss given his breakout 3g/2a form — with his availability for the Argentina QF (Kansas City, Jul 12) still uncertain.
8 Jul 2026: Yakin's post-Colombia comments frame Manzambi as likely out for the Argentina QF specifically, a step down from 'uncertain' — Aebischer/Jaquez situations unchanged.
9 Jul 2026: Denis Zakaria flagged as a suspension risk at right-back (one yellow from a ban) for the Argentina QF; Manzambi/Aebischer/Jaquez situations unchanged from yesterday.

10 Jul 2026: Yakin's language on Manzambi (knee) has hardened further — 'we have hope, but only if it makes sense and there are no risks' — trending more definitively toward missing the Argentina QF, faint hope only for a possible semi; Aebischer/Jaquez unchanged doubts. ESPN/Squawka 'yellow card watch' now groups Zakaria alongside captain Xhaka and Muheim as all one caution from a semi-final ban. Yakin fired back at doubters: 'It's been clear... Argentina is vulnerable... we can challenge the world champion.' Switzerland have never beaten Argentina in 7 meetings (2D 5L), including two prior WC losses (1966, 2014 R16).

10 Jul 2026 (later): a predicted-lineup report upgrades Aebischer, Jaquez and Manzambi from doubts to flatly unavailable for the Argentina QF; Zakaria still pencilled into the back line despite being one yellow from a suspension.

11 Jul: Yakin has formally confirmed Manzambi (knee) misses the Argentina QF; Jashari tipped to deputise again (Sow the more defensive alternative). Aebischer and Jaquez remain sidelined in individual training, unchanged.

Morocco.style: Regragui's 4-2-3-1; compact mid-block, fast transitions; Hakimi captain/RB overlap; Bouaddi elite teen DM; Saibari clever #9; Diaz creative 10; AFCON champions, WC22 semi-finalists
notes: 25 Jun 2026: Dramatic 4-2 comeback vs Haiti. Trailed 1-2 at HT (Bounou OG 10', Isidor 43' cancelled Hakimi 39'). Second half: Rahimi 78', Gessime 89', Saibari 45+1'. 69% possession, 11 SoT vs 2. Great character.
28 Jun 2026: Group C finished 1st (5pts). R32 vs Netherlands. Hakimi key at RB — pace and cross delivery. Diaz-Saibari link key for transitions. Bouaddi DM screens behind. Deep block away from home.

28 Jun 2026: Qualified from Group C as runners-up (7 pts — same as Brazil but inferior GD). Beat Scotland 1-0, blew 2-goal lead but beat Haiti 4-2. Saibari scored 3 group goals from midfield. Hakimi key weapon (overlapping right-back). 2022 semi-final pedigree. Face Netherlands Jun 29 Monterrey 9PM ET. Ezzalzouli and Aguerd out.
29 Jun 2026: Face Netherlands R32 (9AM SGT Jun 30). Hakimi vs Gakpo battle on the right. Brahim Díaz creative hub; low block + transition threat.

30 Jun 2026: R32 vs Netherlands (d 1-1, won pens 3-2): Dominated possession (70%), 11 sh, 5 sot vs Netherlands 6/2. Gakpo broke deadlock (72' counter-attack); Issa Diop equalised (90+1' set-piece). Morocco won shoot-out 3-2. Late set-piece delivery decisive. Diop booked — suspension risk R16 vs Canada ~4 Jul.
4 Jul 2026: Chadi Riad, subbed off with a knock vs Netherlands, has returned to full training and is expected to start at CB vs Canada; Aguerd remains out, leaving the position thin.

4 Jul 2026: Beat Canada 3-0 in R16 M90 (Houston). Saibari off 22' with a suspected hamstring strain — replaced by Rahimi, who scored the third in stoppage time off a Brahim Diaz assist (Diaz's 4th of the tourney, new African WC assist record). Ounahi brace (50', 82') settled it after a slow first half. Halhal, Hakimi, Ounahi, El Khannouss all booked — 4 players now a yellow-card away from a QF ban. Canada become first co-host eliminated. Advance to face France in QF M97 (Boston, Jul 9).

5 Jul 2026: Saibari's hamstring issue still awaiting formal diagnosis (medical staff hadn't met with him as of last report) — treat as doubtful for the France QF pending scan results. FIFA's 2026 rules wipe accumulated yellow cards entering the quarterfinal stage — Halhal, Hakimi, Ounahi and El Khannouss all start the QF with a clean card slate, no suspension risk carried over from R16.

7 Jul 2026: Saibari's scan came back better than feared — described as a minor muscle strain only, with growing optimism in camp he'll be available for the France QF (Boston, Jul 10), a upgrade from the earlier 'doubtful pending diagnosis' read.
8 Jul 2026: Suspension watch — Hakimi, Diop, Ounahi, El Khannouss and Halhal are all one yellow card from missing a semi-final.

9 Jul 2026: Saibari officially ruled out for the France QF per Ouahbi; Chadi Riad now expected to return/be available (no longer a doubt). Hakimi, Diop, Ounahi, El Khannouss and Halhal still carry a yellow card each into the match.

9 Jul 2026: Eliminated in QF, lost 2-0 to France (Mbappé 60', Dembélé 66') despite a standout Bounou display incl. saving an early Mbappé penalty; Saibari (hamstring) missed the match. Tournament run ends at the quarter-final stage.

2 Jul 2026: Beat Bosnia and Herzegovina 2-0 in R32 M81 — Balogun OG (45') then Tillman (82'). Balogun sent off (red) despite scoring the opener via deflection; USA saw out the man-disadvantage. Advance to face Belgium in R16 M94 (Seattle, Jul 7, ~5am SGT).
4 Jul 2026: Balogun's red card vs Bosnia rules him out of the Belgium R16 clash — Pochettino loses his in-form striker (brace vs Paraguay) for the biggest game so far.
5 Jul 2026: McKenzie's knock clarified as foot irritation (not ankle) and he's back in full training. Ricardo Pepi tipped to deputise for the suspended Balogun up top.

5 Jul 2026: Pepi and Wright both tipped as candidates to replace the suspended Balogun up top, with Pepi the more common pick. Tyler Adams enters the Belgium match with a clean card slate (his group-stage yellow does not carry past the first knockout match).
5 Jul 2026: FIFA's Disciplinary Committee suspended Balogun's one-match ban (red card vs Bosnia) on review — he is REINSTATED and available to start vs Belgium, reversing the earlier suspension.
5 Jul 2026: FIFA's Disciplinary Committee suspended Balogun's one-match ban (red card vs Bosnia) on review — he is REINSTATED and available to start vs Belgium, reversing the earlier suspension.

6 Jul 2026: R16 vs Belgium (Seattle, 7 Jul) — Balogun's overturned suspension is the headline, he's in and has 3 goals this WC. McKenzie (foot) and Roldan (muscle) remain doubts and are unlikely to start; Pochettino's plan is to press Belgium's back four and transition quickly through Pulisic and the wide players.

7 Jul 2026: Eliminated 1-4 by Belgium in the R16 in Seattle — Charles De Ketelaere scored a first-half brace (9', 33') either side of Malik Tillman's well-taken free-kick (31'), then Hans Vanaken (57') and a stoppage-time Lukaku finish off Vanaken's pressing sealed it. Belgium outshot the US 15-7 and led on xG 2.15-0.67; McKennie and Tillman were both booked. USMNT's tournament ends in the R16.

notes: 4 Jul 2026: De Bruyne and Doku were both withdrawn early (56') in the Senegal comeback win — worth watching for fatigue management/rotation risk vs USA.

5 Jul 2026: De Bruyne (fully recovered from Oct thigh injury) and Doku (back from paternity leave, 'peak physical condition') both expected to start vs USA — earlier fatigue-management concern from the Senegal game appears resolved.

5 Jul 2026: Debast (leg, out all tournament) had his first training session of the tournament and is fit enough to be an option, but not assured a start. De Bruyne and Doku both confirmed fit and expected to start vs USA; Trossard carrying a minor issue but fit to play.

6 Jul 2026: R16 vs USA (Seattle, 7 Jul) — De Bruyne and Doku both confirmed fit and expected to start; Trossard fit despite a minor issue. Lukaku (33) again expected to start on the bench with an almost-certain second-half cameo. Debast (leg) remains a doubt, unlikely to start even if he makes the squad.

7 Jul 2026: Beat USA 4-1 in the R16 (Seattle) to set up a QF vs Spain in LA — Charles De Ketelaere scored twice in the first half (9', 33'), Hans Vanaken added a third (57') after the US had pulled one back through Tillman (31'), and Lukaku (on for a cameo) finished it in stoppage time off Vanaken's press. Won the shot count 15-7 and xG 2.15-0.67.

7 Jul 2026: QF opponent confirmed as Spain (Los Angeles, Jul 11) after Spain's 1-0 R16 win over Portugal. Spain arrive in form — Yamal's creativity and a miserly Simon in goal (609 minutes without conceding) — while Debast's improving fitness (leg, back in full training) offers Belgium a possible reinforcement at the back.

8 Jul 2026: Amadou Onana ruled out for the remainder of the World Cup after rupturing his ACL in a collision with Pulisic during the R16 win over the USA — a major midfield blow ahead of the Spain QF (LA, Jul 11).
9 Jul 2026: Zeno Debast underwent a late fitness test for the Spain QF; outcome not yet reported — still an unresolved bench/return doubt. Onana's tournament-ending ACL tear (R16) is unchanged.

9 Jul 2026: With Onana out (ACL) and Debast still a fitness question, De Bruyne is tipped to drop into a central-midfield role for the Spain QF to cover the gap; Mechele carries a yellow card (one from a semi-final ban).

10 Jul 2026: Debast situation escalated into a club-vs-federation standoff — Sporting CP blocked his release citing their own medical assessment, overriding Belgium's clearance; a last-minute LA meeting failed to resolve it and he will sit out. De Bruyne recalled into a central creative role to help cover the midfield gap, Doku benched again; Jan Vertonghen named among the subs. Garcia (10 Jul presser): 'Everyone is already talking about us going home... we think we can pull it off,' conceding Spain 'are probably the best at ball possession.'
10 Jul 2026 (later): Garcia confirmed De Bruyne returns to the XI after being benched for the R16 win; with Debast out, back line tipped as Castagne, Ngoy, Mechele, De Cuyper. Onana (ACL) unchanged.

11 Jul 2026: Eliminated in QF, lost 2-1 to Spain (De Ketelaere equalised 41', but Merino's 88th-min rebound won it) despite fighting back from Fabián Ruiz's opener. Compounded by injury misfortune — captain Tielemans withdrew injured in the warm-up, then Courtois exited hurt in the 2nd half (71') forcing backup Senne Lammens into goal for the winning sequence. Tournament run ends at the quarter-final stage.

Ahmed Abou El Fotouh (hamstring) and Hossam Abdelmaguid (head knock) added as doubts alongside existing Salah/Fatouh/Abdelmonem concerns. Hamdy Fathy (groin) back in training but Ramy Rabia tipped to keep his place at CB regardless.
4 Jul 2026: Haissem Hassan and Yasser Ibrahim both booked in extra time vs Australia — either one is a suspension risk if booked again vs Argentina.

5 Jul 2026: Mohamed Salah (hamstring strain, went off 57' vs Iran) has recovered and is fit. Hamdy Fathy and Hossam Abdelmaguid both facing late fitness tests ahead of Argentina — unconfirmed severity, worth checking again before kickoff.

6 Jul 2026: R16 vs Argentina (Atlanta, 7 Jul) — Salah played through his hamstring strain for the full 120 minutes of the Round of 32 vs Australia (won 4-2 on pens after a 1-1 draw) but at times looked reluctant to sprint at full speed; workload being carefully managed into this tie. Fatouh and El Fotouh remain out/doubtful.

7 Jul 2026: R16 vs Argentina (Atlanta) — reports also flag Mahmoud Hafez carrying a knock alongside the confirmed Fatouh/Abdelmonem/El Fotouh doubts; unconfirmed severity, worth checking the team sheet.

7 Jul 2026: Clarifying the earlier vague report — it's Karim Hafez (not a separate 'Mahmoud Hafez') carrying the muscle strain at left-back; he has since trained and is looking likely to be available vs Argentina. Fellow left-back option Ahmed Fatouh remains virtually ruled out with his own thigh injury, so Hafez's fitness is important cover.

7 Jul 2026: Eliminated after a 3-2 R16 loss to Argentina (Atlanta) despite leading 2-0 through Yasser Ibrahim (15') and Mostafa Zico (67') and Shobeir saving a Messi penalty — Argentina's late surge (Romero 79', Messi 83', Enzo Fernandez stoppage-time) turned it around. Karim Hafez, cleared as fit pre-match, played the full 90 minutes. Shoubir, Fathy and Attia were booked. Egypt's World Cup run ends in the last 16.

notes: 12 Jul 2026: Beat Norway 2-1 AET (Hard Rock Stadium, Miami) — Schjelderup opened 36', Bellingham equalised in first-half stoppage time (45+2') and won it 93' pouncing on a Nyland rebound off a Rogers long-range effort; a Heggem goal was ruled out by VAR for a Haaland push on Pickford. Declan Rice, already managing illness/back soreness, was substituted at half-time. Face Argentina in the semi-final, Atlanta, 16 Jul.

12 Jul 2026 (semi-final watch): Declan Rice's hamstring/lower-back issue flared again during the Norway QF and forced a half-time substitution — a fresh fitness doubt for the 16 Jul semi vs Argentina, pending Tuchel's update. Bellingham, Rice, Guehi and O'Reilly are each one caution from a semi-final ban but none were booked vs Norway (only Norway's Ajer was carded), so no new suspension risk. H2H: England lead 3-1 across four WC meetings (1962, 1966 QF, 2002 group) to Argentina's two (1986 'Hand of God' QF, 1998 R16 on penalties) — first-ever WC semi-final meeting between the sides.

12 Jul 2026: CORRECTION — Quansah's red card vs Mexico carries a two-match FIFA suspension, not one; he misses the Argentina semi-final too (ESPN, Fox Sports), not just the Norway QF as earlier reported.

12 Jul 2026 (update): Multiple reports now attribute Declan Rice's Norway QF half-time substitution primarily to a sickness/illness bug rather than the hamstring/back issue alone — outlook described as positive for the 16 Jul semi vs Argentina, pending final fitness confirmation. Guehi (hamstring) and Reece James (hamstring, out since the Ghana group match) have both returned to training and featured in the QF, easing England's backline fitness concerns.

13 Jul 2026: Ezri Konsa's late withdrawal vs Norway was cramp, not a fresh injury — expected to stay in contention alongside Guehi/Stones/James/Spence for the back line. Tuchel clarified Rice's HT sub vs Norway was precautionary given three days ill in bed pre-match, not a fresh knock; aiming for him to be closer to full fitness by the 16 Jul semi vs Argentina. Quansah's red-card ban is CONFIRMED to cover this semi-final (2nd of his two-match suspension).

14 Jul 2026: Declan Rice trending to start alongside Elliot Anderson in the pivot after showing encouraging recovery from his illness bug — England increasingly confident, final call still late. Predicted XI (4-2-3-1): Pickford; James/Konsa, Guéhi, Stones, O'Reilly; Rice, Anderson; Saka, Bellingham, Gordon; Kane.

14 Jul 2026 (confirmed): Declan Rice trained fully Monday in Kansas City — his first full session since the Norway QF — and is now CONFIRMED fit with no remaining fitness doubts to start the semi-final vs Argentina (16 Jul, Atlanta); illness/hamstring concerns fully resolved.

15 Jul 2026: Squad issues continue to ease per Sky Sports — Rice fit and expected to start, clean bill of health bar Henderson (tournament-ending forearm fracture) and the suspended Quansah (serving 2nd match of his ban). Saka has been used as an impact sub through the knockouts and is not expected to go 90 given fitness management, likely keeping Gordon/James-Konsa on the flanks in the predicted 4-2-3-1. Markets have England slight favourites (~-120/-124) over Argentina (~+100/+106), draw ~+185.

16 Jul 2026: Lost 1-2 to Argentina (Atlanta) in the semi-final — Anthony Gordon put England ahead 55' on the break, but a defensive team-shape switch to protect the lead backfired as Messi teed up Enzo Fernandez's 85' equalizer and Lautaro Martinez's 92' header. Elliot Anderson booked (37'). Tournament ends at the semi-final stage; first WC final appearance since 1966 remains elusive.

18 Jul 2026: 3rd-place playoff vs France (Miami, today, 17:00 local) — Reece James a fitness doubt (muscle issue); Jordan Henderson (forearm fracture) and Jarell Quansah (suspended) remain unavailable regardless. Tuchel expected to rotate heavily to manage fatigue and reward fringe squad members. Kane and Bellingham (6 goals each) trail Golden Boot leaders Messi and Mbappé (8 each).

18 Jul 2026: Confirmed/predicted XI for the France bronze final (Hard Rock Stadium, Miami, today) — Reece James ruled out (fresh muscle issue from the Argentina semi); Djed Spence expected to switch to right-back with Nico O'Reilly coming in at left-back (Jarell Quansah, now available after his ban, is the other option). Widely predicted XI: Henderson; Chalobah, Guehi, Stones, Spence/O'Reilly; Mainoo, Anderson, Rogers; Madueke, Kane, Rashford (4-3-3) — Tuchel rotating to manage fatigue and reward fringe squad members; Kane (6 goals) trails Golden Boot leaders Messi/Mbappe (8 each).

19 Jul 2026: Beat France 6-4 (Miami) in a ten-goal thriller to claim third place — Bukayo Saka's hat-trick was the difference despite Kylian Mbappe scoring twice and breaking the all-time World Cup goals record for France. Not tracked in this app's bracket model (the 3rd-place playoff has no bracket match id), so these goals don't feed the Golden Boot Watch panel or matchStats.

h2h.100: Argentina unbeaten in 3 meetings (2W-1D) since 1966, incl. both WC meetings — most recently a 1-0 R16 win in 2014

h2h.201: England lead 3-1 across four WC meetings (1962, 1966 QF, 2002 group) to Argentina's two (1986 'Hand of God' QF, 1998 R16 on penalties) — first-ever WC semi-final meeting between the sides
h2h.300: First-ever World Cup FINAL meeting between the two — and only their 2nd at any World Cup (Argentina won 2-1 in the 1966 group stage). All-time head-to-head is close: 14 meetings, Spain 6-2-6 Argentina. First time the reigning European (Spain, EURO 2024) and South American (Argentina, Copa América 2024) champions have met in a WC final.
